zh.Mercaston, Derbyshire, England: 英國德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾。她於1324年與Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion在英國德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1391年6月29日之後在英國德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓。

zh-hans.Mercaston, Derbyshire, England: 英国德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾。她于1324年与Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion在英国德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1391年6月29日之后在英国德比夏爾梅爾卡斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert was born in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion in 1324, in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 29 June 1391, in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert est née à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion en 1324, à Mercaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée après 29 juin 1391, à Mercaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert wurde in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1324 in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb nach 29. Juni 1391 in Mercaston, Derbyshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert è nata il nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion nel 1324, nel Mercaston, Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 29 giugno 1391, nel Mercaston, Derbyshire, England.

ja.Mercaston, Derbyshire, England: イギリスデルブイシュアイアメルカストン
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Herbertはイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた。1324年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアメルカストンでSir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1391年6月29日以後にイギリスデルブイシュアイアメルカストンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1324년 영국 더비실르 머카스턴에서 Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1391년 6월 29일 이후 영국 더비실르 머카스턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert nasceu em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion em 1324, em Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu após 29 de junho de 1391, em Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert родилась в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion в 1324 г. в местности миркастон, дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 29 июнь 1391 г. в местности миркастон, дирбишир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Herbert nació en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Sir Nicholas I Kniveton Knight of Mercastion en 1324, en Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 29 de junio de 1391, en Mercaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.
